40-eridani-c.claim: Basis states 'A-BC mean separation ≈ 2000 AU. A at apparent V ≈ -9 from C (M_V ≈ 5.9 + distance modulus −15)... a much fainter but still naked-eye orange distant point on a strikingly different scale from B.'
40-eridani-c.source_says: The report's own intro gives the A-BC angular separation as ~83″ at 5.01 pc (→ ~416 AU projected) and an outer period of ~8000 yr (Tokovinin MSC 2018). Kepler's third law with system mass ~1.6 M☉ and P=8000 yr gives a semi-major axis ~470 AU, consistent with the 83″ projection. A separation of 2000 AU would require P~70,000 yr — ~9× the report's stated ~8000-yr orbit.
40-eridani-c.evidence: docs/phase3/40-eridani-c.md:60 (row) and :8-10 (intro '~83″', '~8000-yr outer orbit') and :230-235 (Visual styling '~2000 AU'). Arithmetic: 83″×5.01pc=416 AU; a³=M·P² → a=470 AU for P=8000 yr, Mtot=1.6. At the physically correct ~420-470 AU, M_V 5.9 + distance modulus(−18.2 to −18.5) → V≈−12.3 to −12.6, i.e. comparable to B (V≈−13), not the faint V≈−9 distant point the row asserts. The 2000 AU figure is the only input that yields V≈−9, so the row's rendering conclusion (A much fainter, different scale from B) is reversed under the report's own orbital data.

61-vir-c.claim: 0.235 deg, 'high' confidence, derived = 2 x R*/a x (180/pi) with 0.963 Rsun at 0.2175 AU; prose: 'about half the Sun's apparent diameter from Earth' (docs/phase3/61-vir-c.md:169,252-253).
61-vir-c.source_says: 2*R*/a in degrees = 2*0.963*0.00465047/0.2175*(180/pi) = 2.36 deg (or 2.42 deg with the DB-recommended R*=0.9867 Rsun). The Sun subtends 0.533 deg from Earth, so 61 Vir from c is ~4.5x the Sun's apparent size, NOT half. The stated 0.235 deg is ~10x too small; the formula in the Basis is correct but the output and the comparison prose are wrong.

gj-9066-c.claim: density_g_cc = 0.6, Basis: 'derived: 0.21 M_Jup / (0.85 R_Jup)³ x ρ_Jup ≈ 0.6 g/cc; Saturn-like'
gj-9066-c.source_says: The report's own stated formula evaluates to ~0.45 g/cc, not 0.6: (0.21/0.85³) x ρ_Jup(1.326 g/cc) = 0.342 x 1.326 = 0.45 g/cc. To obtain 0.6 g/cc the radius would have to be ~0.77 R_Jup, contradicting the adopted 0.85 R_Jup. 0.6 g/cc is closer to Saturn's mean density (0.69 g/cc) than the formula result, so the round value appears to be an unstated Saturn-analog pick rather than the cited derivation.
gj-9066-c.evidence: docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:60. Recomputed: 0.21 MJup at 0.85 RJup gives mean density ~0.45 g/cc; Jupiter mean density 1.326 g/cc, Saturn 0.69 g/cc. Low-confidence row (inherits radius low-confidence).

gj-9066-c.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = 35, Basis: 'derived: H = kT/(μ m_H g) with T ≈ 66 K, μ = 2.3, g ≈ 8.9 m/s² (from 0.21 M_Jup, 0.85 R_Jup)'
gj-9066-c.source_says: Surface gravity for 0.21 M_Jup at 0.85 R_Jup is g = 7.2 m/s², not 8.9 m/s² (overstated ~24%). The stated g = 8.9 m/s² would give H = 26.6 km, not 35 km; the reported H = 35 km is instead consistent with the correct g = 7.2 m/s² (H = 32.9 km ≈ 35). So the cited intermediate g is wrong while the final value is roughly right.
gj-9066-c.evidence: docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:71 and prose docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:139. Recomputed: g = G(0.21·MJup)/(0.85·RJup)² = 7.20 m/s²; H(g=7.2)=32.9 km, H(g=8.9)=26.6 km. Medium-confidence row; final value defensible, cited g is the error.

hd-219134-c.claim: dayside_surface_temp_k = 970, with Basis stating the formula 'airless substellar peak T_sub ≈ T_eq · √2 · (1−A)^0.25 ≈ 970 K'. The 970 K value is reused in the Surface synthesis (line 82), Morphology (line 102), Visual styling (line 167), and underpins the ocean_present/surface_ice_caps rows.
hd-219134-c.source_says: The report's own stated formula does NOT yield 970 K. With the report's T_eq(A=0)=789 K (which I confirmed = 788.5 K) and bond_albedo A=0.1: 788.5 × √2 × (1−0.1)^0.25 = 1086 K. Even at A=0 it gives 1115 K. 970 K is ~11% below the formula result (970/1086 = 0.89). The sibling b report shows the identical defect: its stated formula gives 1406 K but it quotes 1250 K (ratio 0.89), so both reports apply an undocumented ~0.89 factor not present in the written formula.
hd-219134-c.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-c.md:46 ('T_sub ≈ T_eq · √2 · (1−A)^0.25 ≈ 970 K'); cross-check docs/phase3/hd-219134-b.md:50 (same formula → quoted 1250 K, computed 1406 K). Numeric: 788.5*1.41421*0.9**0.25 = 1086 K. NOTE: the qualitative 'below basalt solidus ~1300 K, no surface melt' conclusion survives even at the correct 1086 K, so this is not cfg-decisive for the melt narrative — but the decision value itself is inconsistent with its cited derivation.

alpha-centauri-a.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_max = 27.6 (cgs), attributed to 'Robrade 2016 cycle maximum'; report further states alpha Cen A X-ray cycles by a factor of ~4 over the 27.0-27.6 range.
alpha-centauri-a.source_says: Robrade 2016 (1612.06570) gives alpha Cen A's only quoted X-ray luminosity as L_X ~ 6e26 erg/s (log L_X ~ 26.78) at the 2003 detection, then a dimming to XMM non-detection; it explicitly states 'a clear maximum has not been covered yet' for A and the cycle amplitude L_Xmax/L_Xmin is 'nearly one order of magnitude'. The 4e27 erg/s (log 27.6) value in the paper belongs to alpha Cen B (2012 maximum), not A.
alpha-centauri-a.evidence: 1612.06570.md line 31: 'alpha Cen A was clearly detected in the first XMM observation in 2003 with L_X ~ 6 x 10^26 erg s-1'; line 35: 'cycle likely has a period of about 15-20 years ... an X-ray luminosity ratio L_Xmax/L_Xmin of nearly one order of magnitude ... a clear maximum has not been covered yet'; line 51: 'we derive for alpha Cen B an X-ray luminosity of L_X ~ 4 x 10^27 erg s-1'. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:58.

alpha-centauri-a.source_says: Robrade 2016 reports alpha Cen A faded to extreme X-ray faintness with XMM NON-DETECTIONS at minimum (2005-2012), i.e. log L_X well below 27.0; the highest A value the paper states is ~6e26 (log ~26.78). A floor of log L_X = 27.0 is not supported and is inconsistent with the paper's 'nearly one order of magnitude' amplitude from a ~6e26 peak.
alpha-centauri-a.evidence: 1612.06570.md line 31: 'remained extremely X-ray faint from about 2005-2012 ... even non-detections with XMM are present'. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:57 and prose line 102-103 ('log L_X = 27.0-27.6 ... cycles by a factor of ~4').

alpha-centauri-a.claim: visual_in_planet_sky_apparent_diameter_arcmin = 0.5, labeled '(from alpha Cen B at 23 AU mean)', Basis 'derived: 2 R*/a x (180*60/pi)'.
alpha-centauri-a.source_says: The stated formula with the stated 23 AU distance does not yield 0.5 arcmin. For companion B (R=0.8632 Rsun) at 23 AU: 2*0.8632*0.004650/23 rad x 3437.75 = ~1.2 arcmin (for A, ~1.7 arcmin). The value 0.5 is actually the DEGREE figure for the host star seen from a 1.25 AU HZ planet (Visual styling line 151: 'fills 0.5 deg angular diameter'). The Decisions row also contradicts the report's own prose, which puts B's disk at ~0.04 deg (=2.4 arcmin) at periastron - i.e. ~1.2 arcmin at the 23 AU mean, not 0.5 arcmin. Unit (arcmin vs deg) and body are conflated.

eps-ind-a-b.claim: density_g_cc = ~5.3 g/cc; Basis: 'derived: 7.63 M_Jup in a 1.12 R_Jup sphere ~5.3 g/cc'.
eps-ind-a-b.source_says: A self-consistent recomputation of 7.63 M_Jup (1.448e28 kg) in a sphere of 1.12 R_Jup gives ~6.7 g/cc, not ~5.3. (Sanity check: 1 M_Jup at 1 R_Jup with the same constants returns ~1.24 g/cc, consistent with Jupiter's true 1.33, so the method is sound and 5.3 is ~21% too low.) The stated inputs do not produce 5.3.
eps-ind-a-b.evidence: docs/phase3/eps-ind-a-b.md:63 ('7.63 M_Jup in a 1.12 R_Jup sphere ~5.3 g/cc'); my computation rho = M / (4/3 pi R^3) with M=7.63*1.898e27 kg, R=1.12*7.1492e7 m -> 6.73 g/cc.

eps-ind-a-b.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = ~25 km; Basis: 'H = kT/(mu m_H g) with T = 275 K, mu = 2.3, g ~ 15 m/s2-equivalent (~150 m/s2) -> ~25 km'.
eps-ind-a-b.source_says: The basis's own stated inputs do not yield 25 km. With g = 150 m/s2 (the value the basis states it uses), H = kT/(mu m_H g) = (1.381e-23 * 275)/(2.3 * 1.6726e-27 * 150) = ~6.6 km. Even with g = 15 m/s2 it would be ~66 km. No combination of the cited inputs (T=275, mu=2.3, g=150) gives 25 km; the row value is internally inconsistent with its own basis.

gj-896-a-b.claim: surface_gravity_g_earth = ~58, derived: g = G M / R² with M = 2.26 M_Jup, R = 1.1 R_Jup → ~570 m/s² ≈ 58 g⊕ (gj-896-a-b.md:47)
gj-896-a-b.source_says: Computing g = GM/R² with M = 2.26 M_Jup (4.29e27 kg) and R = 1.1 R_Jup (7.69e7 m) gives g ≈ 48.4 m/s² ≈ 4.9 g⊕, NOT 570 m/s² / 58 g⊕. Cross-check: Jupiter's own surface gravity is ~24.8 m/s² (~2.5 g); scaling by M/R² = 2.26/1.21 = 1.87× gives ~46 m/s² (~4.7 g). The report's value is too high by a factor of ~12.
gj-896-a-b.evidence: Self-computed from the row's own stated inputs (M=2.26 M_Jup, R=1.1 R_Jup): g=GM/R²=48.4 m/s²=4.9 g_earth. gj-896-a-b.md:47 states '~570 m/s² ≈ 58 g⊕'. The m/s²→g conversion within the cell is internally self-consistent (570/9.81≈58) but both absolute numbers are ~12× too large.

gj-896-a-b.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = ~9, derived: H = kT/(μ m_H g) with T ≈ 130 K, μ = 2.3, g ≈ 570 m/s² ≈ 9 km (gj-896-a-b.md:56)
gj-896-a-b.source_says: H = kT/(μ m_H g). With the row's stated g ≈ 570 m/s², H = 0.82 km, not 9 km. The value 9 km is only recovered with the CORRECT g ≈ 48 m/s² (gives 9.64 km). So the scale-height value (~9 km) is right, but its stated derivation cites g ≈ 570 m/s² — the same wrong gravity from the surface_gravity row — which is inconsistent both internally (570 m/s² → 0.82 km) and with the surface_gravity row.
gj-896-a-b.evidence: Self-computed: with g=570, H=kT/(μ m_H g)=0.82 km; with g=48.4, H=9.64 km. gj-896-a-b.md:56 cites 'g ≈ 570 m/s² ≈ 9 km'. The correct scale-height value (~9 km) silently assumes g≈48 m/s², contradicting the surface_gravity row (gj-896-a-b.md:47) which asserts ~570 m/s²/58 g⊕.

The 503 K value is Greene 2023's 15-micron eclipse brightness temperature (503 +/- 27 K, quoted as such inside the Ducrot 2412.11627 cache). trappist-1-f.field: atmosphere_surface_pressure_pa / bond_albedo / surface_temp_substellar_k / surface_ice_caps / surface_morphology / ocean_extent (all 'Wolf 2017 1 bar branch' rows) + Surface/Atmosphere prose
trappist-1-f.claim: Report repeatedly states 'Wolf 2017 explicitly shows that f at 1 bar CO2 supports a substellar open-water disk' / 'Wolf 2017 finds 1 bar CO2 is needed for a fully open ocean' and credits the canonical eye-ball-aquaplanet scenario, bond albedo 0.30, substellar open water, and ice-line geometry to the 'Wolf 2017 1 bar branch' (lines 20, 45-46, 49, 51, 59, 62, 88, 129-139, 185, 192, 290).
trappist-1-f.source_says: Wolf 2017 (arXiv 1703.05815, ApJ 839 L1) abstract: 'the outer three planets (f, g, and h) fall beyond the maximum CO2 greenhouse outer edge of the habitable zone. Model results indicate that the outer planets cannot be warmed, despite having as much as 30 bar CO2 atmospheres, instead entering a snowball state.' Wolf 2017 has NO 1-bar open-water branch for f; it concludes f is a snowball at all pressures up to 30 bar. The open-water-at-~1-bar result is Turbet 2018's, and Turbet 2018 (1707.06927 line 402) explicitly states Wolf 2017 'is at odd with our results.'
trappist-1-f.evidence: docs/phase3/_bib/trappist-1-f.yaml lines ~818-832 (Wolf 2017 abstract, &id022, arxiv_id 1703.05815); docs/phase3/_papers/1707.06927.md line 402 ('Wolf (2017), which finds that CO2-dominated atmospheres as thick as 30 bars cannot warm the surface of TRAPPIST-1f and g ... is at odd with our results'); docs/phase3/_papers/1703.05815.md is only a 1-line stub (full text absent).

trappist-1-f.claim: 7000 mSv/yr, confidence 'high', Basis: 'Atri 2019 (1910.09871) for f at 0.037 AU + 1 bar shielding + B-field'.
trappist-1-f.source_says: Atri 2019 Table 4 (with Earth B-field), TRAPPIST-1 f row at 0.037 AU, 1000 g/cm2 (=~1 bar) column = 2.25E-03 Gy ~= 2.25 mSv PER single hard-spectrum event. Without B-field (Table 5) it is 6.66E-03 Gy. The paper reports per-event Gy doses, not an annual mSv/yr rate, and the cited '1 bar + B-field' cell is ~2 mSv, roughly 3000x smaller than 7000. No annual mSv/yr figure for f exists in the paper.
trappist-1-f.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1910.09871.md line 71 ('| TRAPPIST-1 f | 0.037 | 4.28E+00 | 2.92E+00 | 7.76E-01 | 2.25E-03 |', columns are 30/100/300/1000 g/cm2 in Gy/event) and line 84 (no-B-field f row); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:72.

Steffen, Allona Vazan (Rice et al. 2025), title 'Uncertainties in the Inference of Internal Structure: The Case of TRAPPIST-1 f'. trappist-1-f.claim: 3.5, confidence 'high', Basis: 'Garraffo 2017 Fig. 4 bottom panel — 3-4 R_p for f under super-Alfvenic conditions'.
trappist-1-f.source_says: Garraffo 2017 (1706.04617) Section on TRAPPIST-1 f states the headline result is that f has NO magnetopause: 'The extreme wind pressure ... opens the planetary field all the way to the planetary surface ... there is no magnetopause at which the planetary field pressure balances the wind pressure ... The concept of atmospheric protection by a planetary magnetic field does not hold here.' The simple pressure-balance R_mp in Fig. 4 is explicitly described as not applicable to f. Asserting standoff = 3.5 R_p at 'high' confidence contradicts the paper's actual conclusion.
trappist-1-f.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1706.04617.md line 65 ('there is no magnetopause at which the planetary field pressure balances the wind pressure. Instead, stellar wind particles can constantly precipitate directly down open field'); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:70.

trappist-1-f.claim: 0.3, Basis: 'Garraffo 2017 (1706.04617) test case for f at 0.3 G; supports active dynamo' / 'Garraffo 2017 explicitly adopts f as the representative habitable-zone case with 0.3 G surface field'.
trappist-1-f.source_says: Garraffo 2017 examines planetary fields of 0.1 G and 0.5 G ('we examine planetary magnetic fields with equatorial strengths of 0.1 G and 0.5 G that bracket the present day terrestrial value of 0.3 G'). 0.3 G is only the cited Earth reference value the runs bracket; Garraffo did NOT run a 0.3 G test case for f. The magnetosphere section uses 'planetary field is similar to that of the Earth' as an assumption, not a 0.3 G run.
trappist-1-f.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1706.04617.md line 45 ('planetary magnetic fields with equatorial strengths of 0.1 G and 0.5 G that bracket the present day terrestrial value of 0.3 G'); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:68.

A blackbody A=0 T_eq from S=0.373 is ~218 K (Acuna 2021 Table lists f T_eq=218); 215 is a slightly-off derived value mislabeled as an Agol measurement at 'high' confidence.

trappist-1-f.claim: Canonical scenario asserts a substellar open-water lens (ocean_present true, ocean_extent 40 deg) under the 1 bar pick, while the substellar surface temperature row = 260 K, nightside 165 K, global mean 220 K — all below the 273 K freezing point. Prose (line 113) even calls 260 K 'the marginal case for ice/water equilibrium.'
trappist-1-f.source_says: Open liquid water requires T >= 273 K at the substellar point. 260 K is 13 K below freezing, so an open-water lens cannot coexist with the stated substellar temperature. The temperature rows (basis 'Wolf 2017 + 0.1 bar CO2 greenhouse', i.e. the snowball state) describe the 0.1 bar snowball, contradicting the canonical 1-bar open-water scenario the table commits to. Borderline PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE (open water asserted below the water freezing point with no melting mechanism).
trappist-1-f.evidence: docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:46 (surface_temp_substellar_k 260, basis cites '0.1 bar CO2 greenhouse'), :48 (global mean 220), :56-57 (ocean_present true / ocean_extent 40), :113 ('Surface T ~ 260 K is the marginal case for ice/water equilibrium').

trappist-1-f.claim: 0.001-0.005 W/m2, Basis: 'Kislyakova 2017 (1710.08761) — total induction heating 1.1x10^18 W ~= 0.0012 W/m2 normalized to f's surface'.
trappist-1-f.source_says: Kislyakova 2017 Table 1 gives f Q_m = 1.1x10^18 erg/s (not W). 1.1x10^18 erg/s = 1.1x10^11 W; normalized over f's surface (~5.6x10^14 m2) that is ~2x10^-4 W/m2, not the 0.0012 W/m2 stated. The unit is mislabeled (erg/s reported as W) and the per-area normalization is ~6x too high, though the qualitative conclusion (well below molten-mantle threshold) is correct.

tau-cet.field: spectral_type / planet f,g,h omission of tau Cet e
tau-cet.claim: Report prose (lines 39-46, 388-398) frames tau Cet e's absence from the DB as an unresolved 'curation question' — 'Either deliberately dropped ... or an oversight in the Phase 2 ingest' — citing only the Feng 2017 dispute flag and rex-data-comparison, and logs it as an open item to 'decide whether to include e or formally drop it'.
tau-cet.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json:1964 meta_notes definitively records that tau Cet e was reclassified to 'False Positive Planet' by the NASA Exoplanet Archive on 2026-04-09, citing Figueira et al. 2025 ESPRESSO sub-10 cm/s RV non-detection (A&A 700, A174); 'DB retrieval 2026-05-22 correctly inherited e's absence.' The DB thus treats e's omission as a resolved, sourced retraction, not an oversight/open question. The report ignores the False-Positive reclassification and the Figueira 2025 source the DB carries.

trappist-1-d.field: Interior-atmosphere coupling prose (Acuna 2021, not a Decisions row)
trappist-1-d.claim: Atmosphere-synthesis prose (line 156) states Acuna 2021 characterizes d's hydrosphere as 'not in global radiative balance (absorbed flux ~ 33 W/m2 below OLR), implying d is gradually cooling.' Used to justify the preserved 'cooling-and-condensing' cfg variant.
trappist-1-d.source_says: Acuna 2021 explicitly does NOT compute OLR / radiative-balance for temperate planets like d: 'we only calculate the atmospheric parameters (OLR, surface temperature, albedo and thickness) for planets that present their hydrospheres in steam phase' (i.e., b and c only); for temperate planets it 'set[s] the surface temperature in our interior model to their equilibrium temperatures assuming an albedo zero'. proxima-cen-d.source_says: With the report's own insolation S=1.9 (and L=0.00155 L☉, a=0.029 AU), the A=0 equilibrium temperature is T = 278.5 K x S^0.25 = 327 K, not 357 K. To obtain 357 K would require S ~ 2.7. The 357 K value is inconsistent with the stated S/L/a.

proxima-cen-d.claim: equilibrium_temp_k (A=0.3) = 326 K, confidence high, 'derived; rocky bond albedo'
proxima-cen-d.source_says: For S=1.9 and A=0.3 the correct value is T = 278.5*(0.7)^0.25*1.9^0.25 = 299 K, not 326 K. The quoted 326 K is actually the A=0 result (327 K). The two equilibrium-temp rows are mutually inconsistent: the A=0.3 cell carries the A=0 number while the A=0 cell (357 K) matches no consistent S. Intro line 11-12 repeats '326 K (A=0.3)', propagating the error.

proxima-cen-d.claim: Intro (line 14-16) and SM25 Bibliography annotation (line 190-192): 'Suarez Mascareno 2025 §4 confirms a high orbital inclination that places transit geometry outside the photometric monitoring envelope' / 'constrains inclination to high values precluding transits'.

A pole-on stellar alignment might hide the variations...'. The only inclination SM25 uses is the STELLAR rotation-axis tilt 47±7 deg from Klein et al. 2021 (line 382), applied via a coplanarity ASSUMPTION to estimate true masses (0.357 M⊕) — not a planet inclination measurement and not a transit-geometry statement.

proxima-cen-d.claim: Bibliography line 186-187: 'Faria et al. 2022 ... Orbital eccentricity is consistent with circular at the precision of the fit.'
proxima-cen-d.source_says: Faria 2022 (2202.05188) line 126 reports the 5-day signal eccentricity posterior is 'quite wide, with a peak at 0.45' and a median of 0.33 (+0.13/-0.23); circular is only 'not excluded at the 2-sigma level' (line 128). The report's annotation overstates Faria's constraint as 'consistent with circular'. (SM25, not Faria, is the source that statistically adopts a circular model with e<0.25.) The cfg value eccentricity=0 itself matches the DB-recommended SM25 e=0, so the row value is acceptable; the attribution/prose is what is wrong.

proxima-cen-d.source_says: Atri 2020 (1910.09871) reports doses in Gray (Gy), not mSv, and never computes a value for Proxima d: it gives ~1-10 Gy lethal hard-spectrum events, ~100 Gy for extremophiles, '1000s of Gy in extreme cases' for Proxima b (lines 31-33). The report's mSv/yr and mSv/event figures are unstated scaling extrapolations (100 Gy ~= 10^5 mSv, 10 Gy ~= 10^4 mSv are order-of-magnitude consistent), so the magnitudes are plausible but the 'high' confidence and direct Atri attribution overstate paper support; the specific numbers are not in the paper.

hd-219134-h.claim: g = G M / R² with M = 0.34 M_Jup, R = 0.95 R_Jup ≈ 37 m/s² ≈ 3.8 g⊕ (value 3.8)
hd-219134-h.source_says: Self-derivation is arithmetically wrong by ~4x. G·(0.34 M_Jup)/(0.95 R_Jup)² = 9.34 m/s² ≈ 0.95 g⊕, not 37 m/s²/3.8 g⊕. Sanity checks: same formula reproduces Jupiter (24.78 vs known 24.79 m/s²) and Saturn (10.43 vs known ~10.4 m/s²). A Saturn-mass body at 0.95 R_Jup must have Saturn-like ~10 m/s² gravity. 37 m/s² would require R≈0.48 R_Jup, contradicting the report's own 0.95 R_Jup.

hd-219134-h.claim: H = kT/(μ m_H g) with T = 113 K, μ = 2.3, g ≈ 37 m/s² ≈ 35 km (value 35)
hd-219134-h.source_says: The stated value matches neither input. With the report's own (wrong) g=37 m/s², H = 11.0 km, not 35 km. With the physically correct g=9.34 m/s², H = 43.4 km. The 35 km figure is inconsistent with its own cited formula and with the corrected gravity; it inherits and compounds the surface_gravity error.
hd-219134-h.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-h.md:56. Recomputed H=kT/(mu*mH*g)/1000: g=37 -> 11.0 km; g=9.34 -> 43.4 km. Neither equals 35.

proxima-cen.claim: magnetic_dipole_strength_kG = 0.6, high confidence, Basis: 'Reiners 2018 CARMENES Zeeman (dipole component)' (arXiv:1711.06576, 2018A&A...612A..49R).
proxima-cen.source_says: The cached paper 1711.06576 (Reiners et al. 2018, 'The CARMENES search ... High-resolution optical and near-infrared spectroscopy of 324 survey stars') is a spectral-atlas / RV-survey paper. It presents one spectrum per M dwarf and measures RV, Halpha and vsini; it contains NO Zeeman magnetic-field analysis (zero 'kG' occurrences, no mean-field value). The ~600 G field is attributed by Fuhrmeister 2022 to Reiners & Basri 2008 and Klein et al. 2021, not to this paper.
proxima-cen.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1711.06576.md:7 abstract '...we present the survey sample by publishing one CARMENES spectrum for each M dwarf ... we measure its RV, Halpha emission, and projected rotation velocity'; grep 'kG' count = 0 in 1711.06576.md. Cross-check docs/phase3/_papers/2204.09270.md:11 'Proxima Centauri exhibits only a moderate magnetic field of B_f ~ 600 G (Reiners & Basri 2008; Klein et al. 2021)'. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:67.

proxima-cen.claim: magnetic_total_field_kG_rms = 4, high confidence, Basis: 'Reiners 2018 (Zeeman RMS)' (arXiv:1711.06576). Prose (atmosphere synthesis) also says 'Reiners et al. 2018 (CARMENES) measured a Zeeman-broadened mean magnetic field of ~4 kG (RMS over the disk) with a roughly 0.6-kG dipole component'.
proxima-cen.source_says: 1711.06576 (Reiners et al. 2018, 324-star spectral atlas / RV precision paper) contains no Zeeman mean-field or RMS magnetic-field measurement for Proxima (zero 'kG' mentions; only 3 'magnetic' mentions, all instrument/context). The Proxima mean magnetic field measurement originates from Reiners & Basri 2008 / Klein et al. 2021, not this CARMENES catalog paper.
proxima-cen.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1711.06576.md grep 'kG' = 0; line 7 abstract describes RV/Halpha/vsini survey, not magnetography. Fuhrmeister attribution at docs/phase3/_papers/2204.09270.md:11. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:68 and prose docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:102-104.

proxima-cen.claim: activity_cycle_years = 7, Basis: 'Wargelin 2024 X-ray + UV + optical cycle' (arXiv:2411.04252). Prose: '...refined by Wargelin 2024 to ~7 years'.
proxima-cen.source_says: Wargelin et al. 2024 (the cited paper) actually REFINES the cycle to 8.0 yr. Its abstract states the combined data 'now reveals clear cyclic behavior in all three wavebands with a period of 8.0 yr.' The ~7 yr figure is the older Suarez Mascareno 2016 (6.8 yr) / Wargelin 2017 (7.05 yr) result, which the 2024 paper supersedes.
proxima-cen.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2411.04252.md:7 '...now reveals clear cyclic behavior in all three wavebands with a period of 8.0 yr.' (older values quoted at line 15: SM2016 6.8+/-0.3 yr, Wargelin 2017 7.05+/-0.15 yr). Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:64; prose docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:121-122.

proxima-cen.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_flare_peak = 28.5, Basis: 'Fuhrmeister 2022 simultaneous X-ray + FUV peak' (arXiv:2204.09270).
proxima-cen.source_says: Fuhrmeister et al. 2022 report an average flare X-ray luminosity L_X,fl = 6.7e27 erg/s (= log 27.8), with the count rate rising by an average factor of ~10 (range 5-13.5) during flare maxima above a quiescent L_X,qu = 6.6e26 erg/s. No flare X-ray luminosity as high as 10^28.5 (~3.2e28) is reported; 28.5 is ~0.7 dex (factor ~5) above the paper's flare luminosity.
proxima-cen.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2204.09270.md:127 'During flares, the photon count rate increases by factors of 5 to 13.5 ... The average flare luminosity is L_X,fl = 6.7e27 erg s-1.' and 2204.09270.md:121 quiescent L_X,qu = 6.6e26 erg/s. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:66.

proxima-cen.claim: luminosity_lsun = 0.001567, high, Basis: 'derived from R, Teff'. Surface-synthesis prose: '0.00157 L_sun - about 1/640 of the Sun'.
proxima-cen.source_says: Stefan-Boltzmann derivation from the report's own R = 0.141 R_sun and Teff = 2904 K gives L = (0.141)^2 (2904/5772)^4 = 0.00127 L_sun, ~19% lower than 0.001567. The stated value 0.001567 instead matches the measured bolometric luminosity (db recommended Boyajian 2012 = 0.00155 L_sun), so the 'derived from R, Teff' basis does not reproduce the quoted value.

proxima-cen-b.source_says: With the report's own inputs (S=0.646 S_earth per Boutle Table 1, or 0.668 from L=0.00157/a^2) the bare-rock A=0 equilibrium temperature is ~250 K (also ~250-256 K via Tstar*sqrt(R*/2a) with R*=0.141 Rsun, Teff=2904-2980). 226 K would require S~0.43 or a large albedo, neither of which is stated.
proxima-cen-b.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:81; cross-check: Teq = 278.5*(S(1-A))^0.25 with S=0.646 -> 250 K; 1702.08463.md:27 gives S=0.646; db/stellar_props_curated.json Proxima radius 0.141 Rsun, teff 2904.

proxima-cen-b.source_says: For S=0.646 and A=0.3 the equilibrium temperature is ~228 K (~228-234 K via the stellar-radius method). 207 K is ~21 K too low and does not follow from the stated L and a. Note the literature (Anglada-Escude 2016 context) puts Teq ~234 K for Earth-like albedo, also well above 207.

proxima-cen-b.claim: insolation_s_earth = 0.646, Basis 'derived from L = 0.00157 L_sun and a = 0.04848 AU'
proxima-cen-b.source_says: The value 0.646 is correct, but it is NOT derived from L=0.00157/a^2 (that gives 0.668). 0.646 is exactly the stellar-irradiance ratio adopted in Boutle 2017 (881.7 W/m^2 = 0.646 S_earth). The Basis attributes the number to a host-luminosity derivation it does not actually come from; the host L=0.001567/0.04848^2 = 0.667.

proxima-cen-b.claim: density_g_cc = 5.36, Basis 'derived; consistent with Earth-analog rocky composition'
proxima-cen-b.source_says: Density derived from the report's own mass (1.055 M_earth) and radius (1.07 R_earth) is 5.514*1.055/1.07^3 = 4.75 g/cc, not 5.36. The stated 5.36 implies R~1.03 R_earth, contradicting the report's radius_rearth=1.07 row.

proxima-cen-b.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = 11, Basis 'derived: kT/mu*g with T = 260 K, mu = 30, g = 9.0 m/s^2'
proxima-cen-b.source_says: H = kT/(mu*m_H*g) with T=260 K, mu=30, g=9.0 m/s^2 equals 8.0 km, not 11 km. 11 km would need T~357 K or mu~22 at the stated g. The stated inputs do not produce the stated output.

proxima-cen-b.claim: atmosphere_composition = 'N2 95%, CO2 5%, H2O 0.1-1%, trace O2', Basis 'Boutle 2017 simplified N2+CO2'
proxima-cen-b.source_says: Boutle 2017 Table 2 specifies the simplified-N2 atmosphere as N2-dominated with TRACE CO2: CO2 MMR = 5.941e-4 kg/kg (~376 ppmv, present-day-Earth). 5% CO2 is ~130x higher than what Boutle uses. The report's own Surface synthesis (line 124) correctly states '1 bar N2 + 376 ppm CO2', directly contradicting the table's 5%.
proxima-cen-b.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:89 ('CO2 5%') and :166 ('~5% CO2') vs :124 ('376 ppm CO2'); 1702.08463.md:40 'CO2 MMR / kg kg-1 5.941e-4', :43 'nitrogen dominated atmosphere with trace amounts of CO2... values taken from present day Earth'.

proxima-cen-b.claim: magnetosphere_standoff_planet_radii = 1.5, confidence high, Basis 'Garraffo 2022 - stellar wind pressure compresses standoff to < 2 R_p during super-Alfvenic transits'
proxima-cen-b.source_says: Garraffo 2022 explicitly reports the magnetopause standoff for Proxima b 'with values ranging between approximately 3 R_p and 11 R_p, depending on the orbital phase and wind solution' (assuming 0.1 G surface field). It does not give < 2 R_p / 1.5 R_p; the minimum is ~3 R_p.
proxima-cen-b.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:105; 2211.15697.md:83 'magnetopause standoff distance that strongly varies with time, with values ranging between approximately 3 R_p and 11 R_p'.

proxima-cen-b.claim: magnetic_dipole_moment_normalized_earth = 0.1, Basis 'Tie-break: Zuluaga 2018 plausibility range 0.01-1.0; 0.1 selected for partial-shielding regime'
proxima-cen-b.source_says: Zuluaga 2018 abstract gives a MINIMUM dipole moment M_dip > 0.32 (x2.3/div2.9) M_earth (i.e. roughly 0.11-0.74, lower bound ~0.32), not a range '0.01-1.0'. The chosen 0.1 M_earth is below Zuluaga's stated minimum. Garraffo's '0.1 G' surface field (~0.33x Earth's 0.3 G) also corresponds to ~0.33 M_earth, not 0.1.
proxima-cen-b.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:103; 1609.00707.md:7 'the dynamo of Proxima b have a dipole moment M_dip > 0.32 (div2.9, x2.3) M_dip,earth'; 2211.15697.md:77 'planetary surface magnetic field of 0.1 G as estimated by Zuluaga & Bustamante (2018)'.

proxima-cen-b.claim: aurora_emission_species_primary = '[OI] 557.7 nm + N2+ 391.4 nm First Negative + CO2+ doublet', Basis 'Braam 2024 photochemistry framework'
proxima-cen-b.source_says: Braam 2024 (2410.19108) is about spin-orbit-resonance climate dynamics and 3D OZONE chemistry; it contains no aurora, airglow, or emission-line content. Grep for 'aurora', '557', '391', 'Meinel', 'green line' returns nothing in the cached full text. The emission-line identities are generic spectroscopy, not from Braam 2024. (Same attribution issue affects aurora_color_secondary_hex, which cites 'photochemistry of N2+CO2 trace atmosphere'.)
proxima-cen-b.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:112 (and :111); 2410.19108.md:7 abstract describes '3D atmospheric chemistry... 3D ozone distribution', no aurora terms anywhere in cache.

proxima-cen-b.claim: Prose: 'Cloud-band morphology evolves on the 10-day rotation period of the substellar Rossby waves (Cohen 2023), giving in-game cloud animation a visible 1-week cadence.' (supports cloud_morphology / cloud_cover_fraction rows citing Cohen 2023)
proxima-cen-b.source_says: Cohen 2023 Table 3 gives the Rossby-wave cloud-oscillation period for Proxima b as 157.5 days (Control ProxB) / 120 days (Warm ProxB), NOT ~10 days. The report appears to conflate the 11.2-day orbital/rotation period with the 157-day Rossby-gyre oscillation period.

proxima-cen-b.claim: stellar_illumination_color_temp_k = 2980 K, confidence high, Basis 'Proxima Teff' (prose: stellar_color_temp_k = 2980)
proxima-cen-b.source_says: The DB recommended Proxima Teff is 2904 K (Passegger 2019, db/stellar_props_curated.json teff_k=2904), and the host report proxima-cen.md adopts teff_k = 2904 / stellar_color_temp_k = 2904. proxima-cen-b.claim: surface_radiation_dose_msv_yr = 5000, Basis 'Atri 2020 for 1 bar atmospheric shielding + weak B-field; spikes to 10^5 during superflares' (prose cites 'Atri 2020 Table 6')
proxima-cen-b.source_says: Atri 2020 reports doses in Gray PER EVENT (not annual mSv) and its Table 6 is an 'enhancement factor over GCR background', not a 5000 mSv/yr quiescent figure. The paper's quantitative Proxima-b results use 30 g/cm^2 and 1000 g/cm^2 columns and quote per-event Gy (e.g. 1.3e-3 Gy at 1000 g/cm^2 for a 10 GeV hr-event; '1000s of Gy in extreme cases'). The specific 5000 mSv/yr annual value is an interpretive conversion not stated in the cached text; only the column-density basis (~1000 g/cm^2) is directly supported.

Teff = 5344 K'); docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml line 6-7 ('Teff = Korolik 2023 interferometric 5370 K'). hd-69830-b.claim: 5.39 deg, derived: 2 R★ / a × (180/π) with R★ = 0.887 R☉, a = 0.0785 AU
hd-69830-b.source_says: The cell's own stated formula and inputs do not produce 5.39°. 2·0.887·R☉(AU)/0.0785·180/π = 6.02°. With the DB-recommended R★ = 0.9058 R☉ (Tanner 2015) it is 6.15°. 5.39° only results from R★ ≈ 0.794 R☉, which is neither value.
